old_title: Live: Sharks hit with late out as fourth spot goes on the line against Knights
new_title: Storm's injury crisis deepens as star's season ends in loss to Roosters
old_text:
live
NRL updates: Cronulla Sharks vs Newcastle Knights, Sydney Roosters vs Melbourne Storm - live blog, stats and scores
Fourth position on the NRL ladder goes on the line as Cronulla-Sutherland welcomes Newcastle to Shark Park, with the hosts looking to build on last week's mauling of the Dolphins.
Later, the Melbourne Storm will be without some big names as they travel north to face the Sydney Roosters.
Tune in to ABC Sport's live radio call, keep across all the stats in our NRL Score Centre, and follow all the action in our live blog below.
Submit a comment or question
Live updates
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 6:13pm
Sharks vs Knights live score
New: Filters
Choose what information you see below by using filters
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 6:18pm
15' Sharks respond after high kick!
Braydon Trindall lobs up a high kick back across his body.
Ronaldo Mulitalo rises and bats one back, and Will Kennedy is on hand to pick up the loose ball and slide over.
There's a slight delay as the bunker combs through the footage - the suspicion being that Mulitalo knocked the ball on.
But the try is confirmed sooner rather than later.
An easy conversion is nailed by Nicho Hynes.
Sharks 6
Knights 4
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 6:10pm
8' Ponga slides over! Knights in front!
Newcastle makes the most of its early domination and have a deserved lead.
The Knights earn a set restart mere metres from the line, and work the ball to the right, where Kalyn Ponga is lurking, and then finding his way through a couple challenges.
He slides over for the first try of the day.
All a bit too easy there for the visitors.
The conversion is missed.
Knights 4
Sharks 0
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 6:07pm
5' Knights enjoying the best of the early exchanges
The Knights have started this game the stronger, enjoying much of the territory in the opening minutes.
Newcastle bombs a high kick towards the line, but it's a very assured catch by Sione Katoa.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 6:03pm
We're underway at Shark Park
The Sharks, wearing a snappy dark blue and pink number, kick us off.
Newcastle completes a solid first set, crossing well over half way.
The skies have cleared after a day of rain in Sydney, but it's pretty slippery out there.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:50pm
What time do tonight's NRL games kick-off?
Tonight's double header begins with Cronulla-Sutherland vs Newcastle at Shark Park. Here's when the game kicks off across the country and the region.
- 6:00pm AEST: NSW, Queensland, ACT, Victoria, Tasmania, PNG
- 5:30pm ACST: South Australia, Northern Territory
- 4:00pm AWST: Western Australia
- 8:00pm NZST: New Zealand, Fiji
- 9:00pm: Tonga, Samoa
And these are the times for Roosters-Storm clash.
- 8:00pm AEST: NSW, Queensland, ACT, Victoria, Tasmania, PNG
- 7:30pm ACST: South Australia, Northern Territory
- 6:00pm AWST: Western Australia
- 10:00pm NZST: New Zealand, Fiji
- 11:00pm: Tonga, Samoa
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:45pm
What happened last night?
ICYMI, it was a monumental win for the desperate Brisbane Broncos, who kept their finals hopes alive with a surprise victory over the ladder-leading Penrith Broncos.
And it was quite a night for superstar fullback Reece Walsh.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:40pm
Hynes looking to backup record-breaking performance
With AAP
Nicho Hynes was absolutely electric in Cronulla-Sutherland's remarkable 66-0 mauling of the Dolphins last week.
The halfback contributed 30 points, setting a new record for the Sharks, while also laying on three tries.
Coach Craig Fitzgibbon says Hynes is as comfortable as ever next to in-form halves partner Braydon Trindall.
"Most halves are under more pressure than the rest of the team because they're tasked with the delivery of getting a team over the line," Fitzgibbon said.
"If your team's close and you don't tend to win, they tend to be under more pressure than anyone else, and none more so than Nicho."
Fitzgibbon has seen growth in Hynes over his five seasons as coach.
"(Hynes) handles it a lot better nowadays," Fitzgibbon said.
"He's a pretty strong-minded guy and is playing some good footy. We're really comfortable with the way him and 'Tricky' (Trindall) are developing together."
But he wasn't expecting Hynes to reach quite the heights he did against the Dolphins.
"It's a rare day, not everything goes your way like that usually," he said.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:37pm
How the NRL ladder looks heading into the weekend
A spot in the league's top four goes on the line tonight, with the victors at Shark Park moving within four competition points of the ladder-leading Panthers, who suffered a surprise loss to Brisbane last night.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:31pm
Sharks hit with late out but welcome back Origin star
Sharks
Jesse Ramien is a late out for the Sharks, with the centre coming down with illness on game day. That comes after Siosifa Talakai was ruled out with an elbow injury on Thursday. Mawene Hiroti comes into the centres, while Sam Stonestreet and Jayden Berrell join the bench.
In good news for Cronulla, Briton Nikora returns to the team after a post-Origin rest, while Addin Fonua-Blake is back in the starting XIII.
Knights
No late changes for the Knights, with winger Greg Marzhew named despite an injury scare last weekend.
Blues star Bradman Best returns with Fletcher Sharpe moving to five-eighth.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 5:22pm
Welcome
Hello and welcome back to ABC Sport's online coverage of the 2026 NRL season.
Tonight, we will be treated to two massive games, with the Sharks and Knights locking horns in the Shire, before the Storm travel north to face the Roosters.
My name's Henry and I'll be here to guide you through all the action.
Well, what are we waiting for?
Let's get into it!
new_text:
Melbourne Storm's NRL injury crisis deepens in 14-6 loss to Sydney Roosters, Cronulla Sharks hold on for 20-18 victory over Newcastle Knights
The Melbourne Storm have been dealt yet another cruel injury blow in a 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters that leaves the club's NRL finals hopes hanging by a thread.
Warriors recruit Will Warbrick looks to have played his last game for the 10th-placed Storm after suffering a suspected Achilles tendon injury in the first half at Sydney Football Stadium on Friday night.
Storm teammates consoled a tearful Warbrick as he left the field on a medi-cab, having fallen awkwardly in the lead-up to Billy Smith's first-half try.
The winger joins Cameron Munster, Jahrome Hughes, Eli Katoa, Tui Kamikamica, Xavier Coates and Ativalu Lisati in one of the NRL's most high-profile casualty wards.
"Probably didn't realise the severity of it at the time, thought he was just receiving some treatment, but yeah the medi-cab was called, and that's not what you want to see," Melbourne captain Harry Grant said after the game.
"And especially for Will, we know what footy he has been playing for us and he only has a little time left at the club.
"You want to make that enjoyable and memorable and we'll show our support and stay with him now."
The depleted Storm looked in for a tough task facing top four side the Roosters in their first game without either of Hughes or Munster this year.
But Melbourne's defensive grit kept them in the game, even if the Storm were out-muscled through the middle and needed 54 minutes to earn a red-zone tackle.
The Storm threatened to pull off an almighty upset after being gifted rare field position from a questionable high-tackle penalty blown against Nat Butcher, a decision that Roosters coach Trent Robinson labelled as "laughable" after the game.
Down 8-0, the Storm were back within two points when Harry Grant caught the Roosters napping and popped a ball up to Stefano Utoikamanu on the fifth tackle.
Melbourne could have taken an unlikely lead in the final 15 minutes as Trent Toelau put Tyran Wishart into space, but the five-eighth's pass to Sua Fa'alogo was wayward.
The clunky Roosters only breathed easily when towering winger Daniel Tupou crossed on the left edge as the final five minutes approached.
That late drama came after Siua Wong and Smith had crossed in the first half.
Earlier, the Sharks consolidated their place in the league's top four with a 20-18 victory over the Knights.
ABC/AAP
Relive all the action in our live blog below.
Submit a comment or question
Live updates
New: Filters
Choose what information you see below by using filters
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 11:18pm
That's where we'll leave it!
Thanks for joining me for a thoroughly enjoyable Friday night of rugby league action.
Tomorrow, we'll be treated to a triple-header, with the Raiders and Bunnies kicking things off at 3pm AEST.
We hope to see you then!
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 11:15pm
Match report: Sharks consolidate top four spot with Knights win
AAP
Cronulla has cemented its place in the NRL's top four by surviving a late flurry to defeat Newcastle 20-18.
Kalyn Ponga slotted a sideline conversion after winger Greg Marzhew scored with two minutes remaining to give Knights fans a glimmer of hope at Shark Park and send shivers through the Sharks faithful.
But it was all in vain for the visitors, who suffered a second two-point loss in as many weeks after going down to South Sydney 26-24 in round 19.
Tit-for-tat tries in the first half on Friday saw the lead change four times.
The hosts took a 14-12 advantage to the break through the more accurate boot of Nicho Hynes (three from three), while Ponga missed both of his attempts.
That was in keeping with recent form, after Hynes kicked all 11 conversions in the 66-0 demolition of the Dolphins last weekend.
Sheer speed to get around defenders saw Ponga open the scoring on the right edge with the match only eight minutes old, before swinging to the left and attracting enough defenders for Marzhew to cross untouched.
Sharks fullback Will Kennedy and winger Sione Katoa were joined on the try-scorers' list in the second half by Ronaldo Mulitalo, who gratefully accepted a floating Hynes pass and crossed in the corner to push the score out to 20-12, the biggest lead of the night.
Newcastle rallied late and second-rower Francis Manuleleua scooted to the right corner post in the 70th minute, but couldn't ground the ball under a sea of Cronulla defenders.
But the eight-point buffer proved the lifesaver after another Marzhew effort in the 78th minute.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 11:00pm
Robinson slams "laughable" penalty call
Roosters boss Trent Robinson is speaking to the media and is still struggling to wrap his head around the penalty that led to Stefano Utoikamanu's second-half try.
The Roosters' Nat Butcher was pinged for a soft high-tackle penalty, and Robinson says the decision was "laughable".
"How do you find that?" Robinson says.
"Honestly, why would a referee even look at that?
"Like it was laughable, it was completely laughable."
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 10:49pm
Grant reflects on Warbrick's journey with the Storm
Melbourne captain Harry Grant is reflecting on the Storm career of Will Warbrick.
Warbrick is a three-sport international, having represented New Zealand in both rugby sevens and Aussie rules before making his international rugby league debut.
Grant says Warbrick is a committed athlete and will be missed after leaving for the Warriors at the end of the season.
"It's quite remarkable, a real athlete, a real good teammate and a real good club man.
"He comes in every day to get better and the journey, few years in Queensland Cup, plying his trade and really earning the coaches respect and then got his opportunity.
"Along the way, there's been some hiccups for sure, but like I said, he's a really good club man and we're going to miss him."
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 10:39pm
Bellamy and Grant full of sympathy for 'tremendous' Warbrick
Melbourne boss Craig Bellamy is speaking to the media about Will Warbrick, who went down with a season-ending Achilles injury in the first half.
Warbrick is leaving Melbourne for Canberra at the end of this season, and Bellamy says it's an awful way for the winger to end his Storm career.
"It's just a shame for Will to end his career with us on that note," Bellamy says.
"But he's been tremendous for us and I'm sure he'll have great success at the Warriors."
A tearful Warbrick was consoled by a number of teammates as he was taken from the field by a medi-cab.
Melbourne captain Harry Grant says his team will support Warbrick in whatever way it can.
"Probably didn't realise the severity of it at the time, thought he was just receiving some treatment, but yeah the medi-cab was called, and that's not what you want to see," Grant says.
"And especially for Will, we know what footy he has been playing for us and he only has a little time left at the club.
"You want to make that enjoyable and memorable and show our support and stay with him now."
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 10:24pm
Bellamy proud of his team's effort in difficult circustances
Melbourne coach Craig Bellamy is speaking to the media, and is full of praise for his side's effort at the Sydney Football Stadium.
"I was really happy with the players effort for 80 minutes, just a few things we could have done technically or tactically a bit better," Bellamy says.
"But as I just said to them, the effort they put in today was really good.
"There were a few things that didn't go our way today but I couldn't be prouder of our effort."
He says he isn't happy with a number of decisions, including when the siren seemed to sound before the Roosters took a goal line dropout.
"It is what it is and it's not going to change," Bellamy says.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 10:05pm
FT stats
The Melbourne Storm fight hard but ultimately fall short, with the Roosters shading the full-time statistics.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:46pm
FT: Roosters 14 - 6 Storm
The Sydney Roosters get home against a gritty, brave Melbourne Storm outfit.
Already without key players Cam Munster, Jarome Hughes and Moses Leo, the Storm's injury crisis deepened in the first half when a tearful Will Warbrick was wheeled from the arena after sustaining a season-ending Achilles injury.
But, the Storm stood tall after the break, frustrating the high-flying Tricolours and finding a way over the line to set up a grandstand finish.
However, the Melbournians were not aided by two bombed tries, with the Roosters doing just enough to pick up a rare win against an opponent who they so often struggle to break down.
The Bondi club rise to third.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:43pm
77' MEANEY DROPS THE BALL ON THE LINE!
Oh my word, Melbourne miss the chance.
The Storm are working the ball wide on the right and Sualauvi Faalogo looks like he could just charge forward and put the ball down, but instead plays a pass to Nick Meaney to his right.
Meaney drops the ball while crossing the line and it's heartbreak for the Melbourne Storm.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:38pm
75' Tupou scores and that could be that!
With five minutes remaining at Sydney Football Stadium, the Sydney Roosters might have sealed things.
The Roosters work the ball from the centre out to the right, and there's a Tricolour overload as Daniel Tupou is the man to complete the try in the corner.
Sam Walker finally pots a kick and the margin is out to eight points with four minutes remaining.
That could well be that in Sydney.
Roosters 14
Storm 6
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:36pm
71' Storm lose their captain's challenge
Melbourne prop Josh King is pinged for a knock on.
It's a disappointing turnover and looks pretty tight, so the Strom send it upstairs.
After a prolonged review, the original decision stands and the Roosters lose their challenge.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:32pm
64' Big chance Storm!
The Melbourne Storm are looking dangerous and finally have a line break!
It's Tyran Wishart rushing forward with Sualauvi Faalogo to his right.
All the five-eighth has to do is find the right pass - but he can't.
Wishart's ball is far too weak and drops onto the foot of his onrushing fullback, who manages to kick forward and force a line dropout.
Melbourne still very much in this one.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:27pm
63' Nawaqanitawase can't gather
Daly Cherr-Evans leathers one high into the night sky.
Underneath it, Mark Nawaqanitawase is readying himself to spring into action in the corner.
The Roosters flyer rises and makes first contact, but he can't gather cleanly.
The Storm survive.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:19pm
58' The Storm are over from nowhere! Game on!
Oh my goodness, the Melbourne Storm have scored!
Totally against the run of play, on their first meaningful excursion into enemy territory since halftime, Stefano Utoikamanu charges over.
The try comes after some really clever play from Harry Grant from dummy half, who deceives a couple Roosters with a deft shuffle and pass.
The conversion is nailed and, all of a sudden, it's a two-point game!
Roosters 8
Storm 6
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:16pm
52' Melbourne hold firm again!
Wow, this is a seriously inspired rearguard action from the Melbourne Storm.
The Roosters are circling and probing and working the ball out to the right, where Mark Nawaqanitawase gathers and looks destined to slide over.
However, the Storm cavalry arrives in the nick of time, with Tyran Wishart performing the try-saving intervention.
It remains an eight-point game in Sydney.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:12pm
48' More Roosters pressure
The second half has begun just how the second ended, with the Roosters exerting a whole bunch of pressure and the Storm holding firm with serious commitment.
Daly Cherry-Evans bombs a high kick forward and Daniel Tupou loses track of the ball.
It thuds into his back and the Storm survive.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:07pm
A first half to forget for Melbourne fans
Hi Henry, last night's game gives me hope that the Storm can achieve what the Broncos did 🤞
- Tanya
Never mind, my optimism is almost exhausted after the first half and the injuries suffered in it 😔
- Tanya
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 9:05pm
We're back underway in Sydney
The undermanned Storm with all the work to do as the second half begins at the Sydney Football Stadium.
Very, very big ask for the men from Melbourne - but their body language looks good, for what it's worth.
Key Event
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 8:51pm
HT: Roosters 8 - 0 Storm
Sam Walker goes for a field goal on the siren but shanks his effort wide.
Not a great kicking day for the usually dependable Walker, who missed both of his conversions earlier in the half.
But the Roosters were dominant in that opening period, with Siua Wong and then Billy Smith sliding over for the Bondi club.
The undermanned Storm, however, have done well to restrict the Roosters to just those two scores, defending with real grit in the face of some pretty slick Tricolour play.
But the real story of that half were the tears of Will Warbrick.
The winger was helped off with the assistance of a motorised cart after sustaining what will surely be a season-ending injury.
An awful moment for Warbrick and more injury agony for the already depleted Melbournians.
Fri 17 Jul 2026 at 8:45pm
38' More injury worries for the Storm
Alec MacDonald copped a nasty head knock in the tackle and blood is streaming from his brow.
He's up and walking and looking okay (minus the claret) but is off for an HIA.

Sharks, while also laying on three tries. Coach Craig Fitzgibbon says Hynes is as comfortable as ever next to in-form halves partner Braydon Trindall. "Most halves are under more pressure than the rest of the team because they're tasked with the delivery of getting a team over the line," Fitzgibbon said. "If your team's close and you don't tend to win, they tend to be under more pressure than anyone else, and none more so than Nicho." Fitzgibbon has seen growth in Hynes over his five seasons as coach. "(Hynes) handles it a lot better nowadays," Fitzgibbon said. "He's a pretty strong-minded guy and is playing some good footy. We're really comfortable with the way him and 'Tricky' (Trindall) are developing together." But he wasn't expecting Hynes to reach quite the heights he did against the Dolphins. "It's a rare day, not everything goes your way like that usually," he said.
